Assembly: The assembly jhSerGayi1.hap1.1 is based on 28x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The order and orientation of contigs along chromosome 6 is uncertain between 8.7 Mb and 9.3 Mb.

Assembly: The assembly idEmpLivi1.1 is based on 26x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of scaffolds within the centromeric regions of Chromosome 1 ~33.93-38.37, Chromosome 2 ~19.15-27.67, Chromosome 3 ~20.02-29.19, Chromosome 4 ~18.64-21.39, and Chromosome 5 ~25.74-29.22Mb is unsure. The sample appears to be from the heterogametic sex. The X chromosome was assigned based on read coverage statistics. A Y is likely to be collapsed and reside within the unplaced scaffolds but could not be confidently assigned.

Assembly: The assembly ilEpiScut1.hap1.1 is based on 90x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly of Haliotis ovina (xgHalOvin1) is based on 40X PacBio data and 165X Arima Hi-C data generated as part of the ATLASea programme (https://www.atlasea.fr).The assembly process included the following steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. In total, 219 contigs were identified as contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral), totaling 38 Mb (with the largest being 2 Mb). Additionally, 220 regions totaling 61 Mb (with the largest being 7.2 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using oatk. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 10 supplementary haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 8.3 Mb (with the largest being 1.5 Mb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly drAlcFaer1.hap1.1 is based on 31x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The species shows a high chromosome number and different chromosome distribution patterns. In addition, it has a many rearrangements and hybridisation events history. The number of chromosomes may vary within the species. It was not possible to infer ploidy. The haplotypes could not be separated during curation, so the complete set of chromosomes was submitted.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).

Assembly: The assembly ilHelSaph4.1 is based on 78x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Chromosomes in the alt assembly are named after their homolog in the primary assembly. Z chromosome identified based on synteny with Heliconius sara (GCA_917862395.2).

Assembly: The assembly dcCheAlbu1.1 is based on 53x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).g The Z and W chromosomes were identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 18 (4,429 - 4,709c Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 26 (5,341 - 5,692 Kbp) are unknown.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Diptera; Nematocera; Sciaroidea; Sciaridae; Bradysia
Assembly: The assembly idBraCopr2.1 is based on 59x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. A manually phased assembly (based on HiC signal) was generated from a jointly curated primary and alt assembly. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. GRC1 and GRC2 are germline restricted chromosomes. GRC naming is based on coverage patterns identified in https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pbio.3001559.

Assembly: The assembly iHelSar1.2 is based on 50x PacBio data, 294x coverage 10X Genomics Chromium data, and 111x coverage Dovetail Genomics Hi-C data from a different individual iHelSar2 (BioSample SAMEA8947139) generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with wtdbg2, retained haplotig identification with Purge Haplotigs, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. The assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL, Hi-C based scaffolding was carried out with SALSA2, and finally there was another round of manual curation. Chromosome-scale scaffolds are named by synteny to the Heliconius melpomene melpomene assembly Hmel2.5 in LepBase.

Assembly: The assembly icOtiRugo1.hap1.1 is based on 152x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The scaffolds order and orientation are uncertain on Chromosome 25 from 6.50 to 6.98 Mbp, Chromosome 27 from 3.50 to 3.67 Mbp and on Chromosome Y from 266 to 730 kbp. The sex chromosomes were assigned based on the copy number on the diploid assembly. It looks to have happened many chromosomal fissions in Otiorhynchus rugosostriatus genome when compared to other Curculionidae family beetles.
